title: Fragaria norvegica
intro: Fragaria norvegica, also known as the Norwegian strawberry, is a perennial plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to northern Europe and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Fragaria norvegica include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and ellagic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Fragaria norvegica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and infections.
taxonomy: Fragaria norvegica is a member of the family Rosaceae, which includes other popular fruits like apples and pears. This plant is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Fragaria. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Within the genus Fragaria, Fragaria norvegica is closely related to other species such as Fragaria vesca and Fragaria x ananassa. Fragaria norvegica is a perennial herbaceous plant that produces edible strawberries.
uses: Fragaria norvegica, also known as the Norwegian strawberry or wild strawberry, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. Fragaria norvegica is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. In addition, the plant has been used to treat coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Fragaria norvegica.
benefits: Fragaria norvegica, also known as the Norwegian strawberry or wild strawberry, offers various health benefits due to its rich nutritional content. Rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, it has been traditionally used to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may also contribute to its potential anti-cancer properties.

constituents: Fragaria norvegica is a species of strawberry native to Europe.  It contains a variety of bioactive compounds including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The active constituents in Fragaria norvegica may also include anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other polyphenolic compounds.
